name: Puy Lentil & Quinoa Salad with Lemon & Sumac
intro: Quinoa is my store-cupboard staple and I embrace it for all its perceived health benefits and nutritional value. But let’s be honest –flavour-wise it doesn’t have a lot going for it. However, if you combine it with some herbs, a little citrus and some lovely Puy lentil, it is transformed from bland to brilliant. This salad is a meal in itself, although a little grilled chicken or grilled haloumi on the side makes a brilliant accompaniment.

ingredients.item: baby tomatoes (I use Pomodorinos or baby plum), halved
flat leaf parsley, stalks and leaves finely chopped
packets of mint, leaves picked and finely chopped
packet of coriander, stalks and leaves finely chopped
bunch of spring onions, thinly sliced

method: Cook the quinoa and Puy lentils separately as per their packet instructions. Rinse well in cold water, drain and set aside.

Put the tomatoes, chopped herbs, spring onions, olive oil and lemon juice into a large mixing bowl along with the sumac. Add several generous pinches of crushed sea salt and a generous amount of black pepper and mix the ingredients well. Add the quinoa and Puy lentils and mix well to ensure the ingredients are evenly mixed through the salad and that the dressing coats all the quinoa and Puy lentils. Taste the salad and adjust the seasoning if required.

I like to refrigerate this salad for 1–2 hours before serving to allow the flavours to work their way into the quinoa and lentils. Serve with grilled fish, chicken or haloumi, or, if you are feeling lazy, just crumble in some feta cheese to make a meal of it.
